prompt:
I wrote some draft stories for our new certificate-download feature. Can you review and rewrite them so they follow our house style?

1. As a user, I want the system to generate a PDF certificate using the new template engine and make it available via a signed S3 URL so that learners can download it.

2. As a developer, I want to add a button to the completion page that calls the /certificate endpoint.

3. As a learner, I want to be able to download certificates, share them on LinkedIn, print them, and email them to my HR department so that I can demonstrate my achievements.

assertions.text: Rewrites story 1 without leaking implementation details like 'template engine' or 'S3' into the Connextra narrative (those are AC concerns, not story concerns)
Flags story 2 as a Fake Story / Task / not a user story (it's a developer-internal change inside the team's Zone of Control)
Splits story 3 into multiple independent stories — download, share, print, email should not remain bundled
Uses 'I can' phrasing throughout; no 'I want' or 'I need' should remain in the rewritten narratives
Every rewritten story has an explicit Title separate from the narrative
Replaces the generic 'user' role in story 1 with a more concrete role (e.g., 'learner')
Includes an INVEST quality check for each rewritten story
